explanation:
The **DIY home and construction approach** relies on proven efficiency standards (square meters per gallon of paint, brick per square meter of wall, etc.). Based on industry best practices and international heating standards.

## How It's Calculated

Boiler power sizing: 100W/m² for standard room conditions. Central heating system sizing. Free and fast.

This calculator applies the formula with your values, checking them against typical ranges and alerting when results fall outside normal parameters.

## Important Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For major decisions (heating system upgrades, replacements, or new installations), consult a licensed HVAC professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What size boiler do I need for my room?
faq.a: Use the 100W/m² standard: multiply your room's square meters by 100, then convert to kW. Adjust upward for poor insulation or cold climates.

faq.q: How accurate is this boiler calculator?
faq.a: Results are practical estimates with ±5-10% accuracy depending on insulation, local climate, and system type. Always consult an HVAC pro for final sizing.

faq.q: Does this account for climate differences?
faq.a: The base formula uses the standard 100W/m² for typical conditions. Adjust upward in colder regions or for older homes with poor insulation.

faq.q: What heating standards does this follow?
faq.a: Based on proven efficiency ratios used in central heating system design and international building codes.
Yes, completely free with no registration or signup required.
Yes. All calculations run in your browser—we don't send any data to our servers.

faq.q: Can I use this for radiant floor heating?
faq.a: This is designed for forced-air and water-based central heating. Radiant systems use different sizing methods.

faq.q: What if my result seems wrong?
faq.a: Double-check your room area and adjustment factor. Consider insulation quality, ceiling height, and exterior walls. An HVAC professional can refine your estimate.
